argument_substitution.mechanism: $ARGUMENTS substitution in Markdown-type commands; equivalent to Claude Code's $ARGUMENTS pattern; Executable commands receive args via shell process stdin/args

builtin_commands.mechanism: no built-in slash commands documented; Factory commands are user-defined Markdown templates or executables

providerExtensions.name: Two Command Types
providerExtensions.description: Commands come in two types: Markdown (.md files — prompt templates injected into the conversation, support $ARGUMENTS substitution) and Executable (scripts — run as shell commands with stdout fed into the conversation). Both share the same discovery directories.

providerExtensions.name: Command Discovery Paths
providerExtensions.description: Commands are discovered from .factory/commands/ (project) or ~/.factory/commands/ (personal). The filename (without extension) becomes the slash command name. Project commands override personal commands with the same slug.

providerExtensions.name: Skills Supersede Commands
providerExtensions.description: Factory's own docs note that skills are now user-invokable via slash commands and recommend skills over commands for new work. Commands remain supported for backwards compatibility but are considered a legacy mechanism relative to skills.
